**SPARXSTAR**

**Repository Documentation Standard**

copilot-instructions.md  ·  AGENTS.md  ·  INSTRUCTIONS.md

*Merged from Copilot-generated code knowledge \+ Platform Integrity Map architectural law*

**Version 1.0  ·  Starisian Technologies**

**CONFIDENTIAL  ·  PATENT PENDING**

# **Part 1 — The Three Files and What Each Does**

Every SPARXSTAR repository contains three documentation files. They serve different readers at different moments. They must be consistent with each other but they are not identical.

| File | Read by | Purpose | Length |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| .github/copilot-instructions.md | GitHub Copilot (automatically, every session) | Architectural law \+ code mechanics. What to do, what never to do, where things live. | Concise. Every line earns its place. |
| AGENTS.md | Claude Code, other AI agents, MCP-aware tools | Full reasoning, cross-repo context, the why behind the rules. More discursive than copilot-instructions. | Comprehensive. Agents need reasoning. |
| INSTRUCTIONS.md | Human developers onboarding to this repository | Setup, workflow, how to contribute, how to run tests. The human entry point. | Complete. Humans need context. |

All three files must be updated together when architecture changes. A change that updates copilot-instructions.md but not AGENTS.md creates drift. Drift creates bugs.

# **Part 2 — Sky DVE Core — All Three Files**

The Sky files below merge Copilot's code-derived knowledge (from actual working code) with the Platform Integrity Map architectural law. Neither source alone is complete. Together they give any AI agent or human developer everything they need to work in this repository without violating the platform architecture.

**☁  Sky DVE Core — .github/copilot-instructions.md**

File path: .github/copilot-instructions.md

| \# SPARXSTAR — sparxstar-sky-dve-core \# Copilot Instructions \#\# What this repository is Sky is the Sovereign Knowledge Execution Core — the AI intake layer that converts conversation into structured, committed knowledge. It is the voice of the Elder in the room. It listens. It extracts meaning without inventing it. It accumulates a structured draft. It asks exactly one clarifying question when something is missing. It waits. Nothing happens until the human presses a button. This is also the Press Room engine. The Digital Village Elder and the musician building a press campaign sit across from the same listener. The architecture does not distinguish between them. \#\# Thin-waist architecture   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐  ← Future / flexible   │  SCF schemas · AI providers · Helios / MPC  │   ├──────────────────────────────────────────────┤  ← THE SYSTEM (this repo)   │  Ability contract · Draft lifecycle          │   │  Commit Gate · Session model                 │   ├──────────────────────────────────────────────┤  ← Stable / unchanging   │  WordPress posts · Database · Persistence    │   └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘ The middle layer is explicit, enforced, and test-covered. Everything else (SCF schemas, AI providers, wallets, royalties, mobile) plugs into it without touching the middle layer. \#\# Core sovereign model   AI suggests → system validates → human commits \- AI extracts and asks questions. PHP enforces workflow. \- Nothing exists before /commit. Everything exists after it. \- Running commit twice does nothing new (idempotent). \- Session lock and UUIDs assigned ONLY on that explicit human action. \#\# Ability contract — seven mandatory keys   'extract\_event' \=\> \[       'name'            \=\> 'extract\_event',       'title\_field'     \=\> 'title',       'asset\_field'     \=\> 'description',       'post\_type'       \=\> 'post',       'required\_fields' \=\> \['title', 'date', 'description'\],       'optional\_fields' \=\> \['location'\],       'schema'          \=\> \[ /\* JSON Schema for Anthropic tool \*/ \],   \] title\_field → post\_title. asset\_field → post\_content. All other fields → update\_post\_meta(). Adding ability \#2: add ONE entry to ability\_registry(). Touch no other file. \#\# Key invariants — never break these \#\#\# 1\. AI fill-gap rule (draft merge)   // Confirmed fields survive AI turns — human truth is never overwritten   if ( in\_array( $key, $confirmed, true ) ) { continue; }   // Only fill gaps — never overwrite existing extracted values   if ( \! array\_key\_exists( $key, $existing ) || "" \=== $existing\[$key\] ) {       $existing\[$key\] \= $value;   }   NEVER use isset() here. isset() returns false for null keys.   array\_key\_exists() is required — platform-wide rule. \#\#\# 2\. Asset / metadata split (commit gate)   $post\_title   \= $draft\[$ability\['title\_field'\]\];   $post\_content \= $draft\[$ability\['asset\_field'\]\];   foreach ( $draft as $key \=\> $value ) {       if ( $key \=== $asset\_field || $key \=== $title\_field ) { continue; }       update\_post\_meta( $post\_id, sanitize\_key($key), sanitize\_text\_field($value) );   } \#\#\# 3\. Transactional commit gate   $wpdb-\>query( 'START TRANSACTION' );   // wp\_insert\_post \+ update\_post\_meta \+ session lock   $wpdb-\>query( 'COMMIT' );   // on failure: $wpdb-\>query( 'ROLLBACK' ); \#\#\# 4\. Idempotent commit   if ( \! empty( $session-\>post\_id ) ) {       return existing\_post\_response(); // never reaches wp\_insert\_post()   } \#\#\# 5\. Ownership enforcement on every write   // In chat(): caller-supplied owner\_uuid — NEVER $session-\>owner\_uuid   $this-\>sessions-\>assert\_writable( $session, $owner\_uuid );   // In commit():   if ( $session-\>owner\_uuid \!== $owner\_uuid ) { return 403; }   Passing $session-\>owner\_uuid as the second argument trivially succeeds   and bypasses the ownership check. Never do this. \#\# Platform rules (from Platform Integrity Map) \- Session \= wp\_spx\_sessions table ONLY.   NEVER WordPress $\_SESSION or auth cookies. \- Sky MUST NOT perform identity checks outside HeliosClientInterface. \- Asset fields are IMMUTABLE after first write.   Modifications produce \_derived fields. Original untouched. Ever. \- The AI prompt is fixed across all abilities. Never change it per user. \- Loads SCF schema \+ Block schema for Abilities.   Does NOT load Policy schema (that is Mehns). \- permission\_callback \=\> "\_\_return\_true" is INTENTIONAL.   Auth is enforced at the edge by Helios before requests reach WordPress.   Do not add WordPress auth here. \- Draft encryption: all draft payloads AES-256 encrypted before DB write.   Plaintext draft write \= DraftEncryptionException. \#\# File responsibilities   class-spx-db.php      — dbDelta schema for wp\_spx\_sessions   class-spx-session.php — Repository: get, create, update\_draft, lock, assert\_writable   class-spx-draft.php   — Stateless draft logic: merge, missing, confirm   class-spx-ai.php      — Anthropic API wrapper \+ ability registry   class-spx-api.php     — Four REST endpoints under spx/v1   spx-sky-engine.php    — Bootstrap: activation, file loading, REST registration   assets/app.js         — Zero-dependency two-panel UI \#\# REST endpoints   POST /wp-json/spx/v1/session        — create session; returns session\_uuid \+ owner\_uuid   POST /wp-json/spx/v1/chat           — send conversation turn; accumulate draft   POST /wp-json/spx/v1/commit         — Commit Gate: create WP post inside transaction   GET  /wp-json/spx/v1/session/{uuid} — read current session state \#\# Schema loading   Sky Abilities load: SCF schema \+ Block schema   Reference: sparxstar-digital-village-elder-schema (branch max)   Sky does NOT load Policy schema — that belongs to Mehns. \#\# Dependencies — from sparxstar-ouroboros-integrity   HeliosClientInterface — all identity/session checks route through this   ValidationException, DraftEncryptionException   AgreementResult, ResourceSensitivity   ValidationHelper including normalize\_unicode\_nfc()   Never redefine these symbols. Use the Ouroboros package. \#\# Build and test   composer install   composer test       \# PHPUnit — 32 tests, 63 assertions   composer lint       \# PHP\_CodeSniffer   npm run build       \# assets   npm run lint        \# ESLint \+ Stylelint \#\# The next PR must NOT touch   commit() logic   Session model (SpxSessionRepository, wp\_spx\_sessions schema)   Draft merge logic (SpxDraftService)   REST API routes or response shapes   If a future PR touches those files to support a new ability,   the architecture is broken. All ability-specific behaviour   must flow through the seven contract keys. \#\# When uncertain   Writing to the database? → wp\_spx\_sessions (draft) or Dheghom DAL (governed)?   Checking identity? → HeliosClientInterface only.   Validating a field? → ValidationHelper from Ouroboros.   Adding a new ability? → One entry in ability\_registry(). Nothing else. |
| :---- |

**☁  Sky DVE Core — AGENTS.md**

File path: AGENTS.md

| \# SPARXSTAR Sky DVE Core — Agent Instructions \#\# Who reads this file This file is for AI agents operating in this repository: Claude Code, GitHub Copilot in agent mode, and any MCP-aware tool. It provides deeper reasoning than copilot-instructions.md. Read both. This file explains the why. The other file states the what. \#\# Platform context This is one of seven repositories in the SPARXSTAR sovereign knowledge architecture. The execution order is:   Ouroboros → Helios → Sirus → Sky → Mehns → Dheghom Sky is layer 3\. It receives context from Sirus via the ContextPulse, is gated by Helios agreement, accumulates drafts in wp\_spx\_sessions, and hands committed payloads to Dheghom via the Mehns governance sieve. Read the Platform Integrity Map before making architectural decisions. It is the normative superspec. All rules here derive from it. \#\# The hostile environment assumption This plugin runs in a full commercial WordPress ecosystem alongside WooCommerce, SEO plugins, post grids, AI plugins, and arbitrary helpers. Any of them may write to wp\_postmeta with any key, query any post, or hook into WordPress at any priority. This means: governance guarantees must be enforced by architecture, not by plugin etiquette. Do not assume other plugins will cooperate. If a guarantee depends on other code behaving, it is not a real guarantee. \#\# Why the session is a custom table wp\_spx\_sessions exists because WordPress post meta is too heavy for rapid iterative JSON merging during a live conversation loop. Every exchange in the chat hits the database. That loop must be fast, lightweight, and entirely decoupled from the WordPress post lifecycle until the Commit Gate fires. The custom session table handles the conversation. WordPress posts handle the artifact. They never meet until commit. \#\# Why permission\_callback is \_\_return\_true Helios enforces authentication at the edge (Cloudflare Worker) before any request reaches WordPress. By the time a request arrives at these REST endpoints, Helios has already evaluated the ContextPulse, verified the proof, and either allowed or denied the request. Adding WordPress auth here would be redundant at best and would create a second, inconsistent auth path at worst. Do not add WordPress auth. \#\# Why confirmed fields use array\_key\_exists not isset isset() returns false for null keys — it cannot distinguish between "this key does not exist" and "this key exists but has the value null." In a system that holds indigenous languages where null and absence mean different things, that ambiguity is unacceptable. array\_key\_exists() is the platform-wide standard. This is enforced by Platform Integrity Map Rule 9.1. \#\# Why the asset field is immutable The asset field is what the human gave — the elder's words, the musician's recording, the contributor's story. It is cryptographically sacred. It is the legal backbone of the system's promise to every contributor: what she gave is what is stored. Break this once and trust collapses permanently. Any modification creates a \_derived field. The original is never touched. \#\# Why the ability contract has exactly seven keys The thin-waist principle: the middle layer (ability contract, draft lifecycle, commit gate, session model) must be stable and unchanging. The seven-key contract is the interface between the flexible top layer (SCF schemas, AI providers, new content types) and the stable bottom layer (WordPress posts, database, persistence). If you need to support a new content type and you find yourself changing commit() or the session model, the new content type should be expressed as a new ability definition — not as a change to core logic. \#\# Cross-repo dependencies This repository depends on sparxstar-ouroboros-integrity for: \- HeliosClientInterface: all identity and session checks route here.   Search for this interface in the Ouroboros repo before implementing   any identity logic. \- ValidationHelper: includes normalize\_unicode\_nfc() which is   mandatory for all linguistic fields (Platform Integrity Map Rule 9.8). \- ValidationException, DraftEncryptionException: never redefine these. \- AgreementResult, ResourceSensitivity enums: never redefine these. When the MCP token is configured, you can search the Ouroboros repo directly to find the current interface signatures. \#\# What to do before writing any new code 1\. Check: does the symbol I need already exist in Ouroboros? 2\. Check: does adding this require touching commit(), the session model,    or draft merge logic? If yes, express it as an ability key instead. 3\. Check: am I writing a direct $wpdb call on a governed field?    If yes, route through the DAL instead. 4\. Check: am I assuming WordPress auth? Helios handles that. \#\# Known safe extension points (do not pre-optimize)   Multiple asset fields  → extend asset\_field to asset\_fields\[\];                            keep asset\_field as v1 alias   Nested draft data      → draft\_data is JSON; SpxDraftService::merge()                            can be extended to recurse   Multiple actors/roles  → add contributors\[\] to session;                            owner\_uuid remains write authority   SCF field validation   → add field\_types key to ability contract;                            commit() reads it generically Do not add these in the current PR unless they are required. Extending the thin waist prematurely pollutes the architecture. |
| :---- |

**☁  Sky DVE Core — INSTRUCTIONS.md**

File path: INSTRUCTIONS.md

| \# SPARXSTAR Sky DVE Core — Developer Instructions \#\# What this is Sky is the conversational intake layer of the SPARXSTAR platform. It converts natural conversation into structured, committed knowledge through an AI extraction engine, a draft accumulation layer, and an explicit human commit action. Read this file to understand how to set up, develop, and contribute to this repository. Then read AGENTS.md for the architectural reasoning. Then read .github/copilot-instructions.md for the specific rules. \#\# Prerequisites   PHP 8.2+   WordPress 6.8+   Composer 2.x   Node.js 20+   Redis (required for trust-state continuity)   MariaDB 10.6+ or MySQL 8.0+ \#\# Setup   1\. Clone the repository into wp-content/plugins/spx-sky-engine/   2\. Install PHP dependencies:        composer install   3\. Install JS dependencies:        npm ci   4\. Build assets:        npm run build   5\. Add to wp-config.php:        define( 'SPX\_AI\_KEY', 'your-anthropic-api-key' );   6\. Activate the plugin in WordPress admin.      The plugin creates wp\_spx\_sessions on activation via dbDelta().   7\. Verify the loader fires correctly:      mu-plugins/00-sparxstar-loader.php must load this plugin      as part of the platform boot sequence. \#\# Running tests   composer test          \# full suite: lint \+ analyze \+ PHPUnit   composer run lint      \# PHP\_CodeSniffer (PSR-12)   composer run analyze   \# PHPStan Level 5   composer run test:unit \# PHPUnit only   npm run lint           \# ESLint \+ Stylelint   npm run build          \# production asset build \#\# Test coverage   tests/unit/sky-engine/SpxDraftServiceTest.php     — 7 tests: merge/missing/confirm edge cases     — tests array\_key\_exists vs isset behavior   tests/unit/sky-engine/SpxAITest.php     — 8 tests: ability registry shape, title\_field     — test\_every\_ability\_satisfies\_the\_factory\_contract()       calls SpxAI::get\_ability\_names() dynamically and asserts       all seven contract keys are present on every registered ability.       A future ability missing a key fails CI immediately.   tests/unit/sky-engine/SpxSessionRepositoryTest.php     — 4 tests: assert\_writable ownership and lock logic \#\# Adding a new ability   1\. Open includes/class-spx-ai.php   2\. Add one entry to ability\_registry() with all seven mandatory keys:      name, title\_field, asset\_field, post\_type,      required\_fields, optional\_fields, schema   3\. Run composer test — the factory contract test will verify your entry   4\. That is it. Touch no other file. \#\# Branching and PR rules   No direct commits to main.   All changes via feature branch \+ PR.   CI must be green before merge:     \- PHPStan Level 5 passes     \- PHP\_CodeSniffer passes (PSR-12)     \- ESLint \+ Stylelint pass     \- PHPUnit passes (all 32 tests) \#\# File structure   spx-sky-engine/   ├── spx-sky-engine.php          ← bootstrap, constants, activation hook   ├── includes/   │   ├── class-spx-db.php        ← dbDelta, wp\_spx\_sessions schema   │   ├── class-spx-api.php       ← REST route registration   │   ├── class-spx-ai.php        ← Anthropic wrapper \+ ability registry   │   ├── class-spx-session.php   ← session repository   │   └── class-spx-draft.php     ← draft merge logic   └── assets/       └── app.js                  ← two-panel UI \#\# REST API   POST /wp-json/spx/v1/session        — create session   POST /wp-json/spx/v1/chat           — conversation turn \+ draft update   POST /wp-json/spx/v1/commit         — Commit Gate: transactional post creation   GET  /wp-json/spx/v1/session/{uuid} — read session state   Note: permission\_callback is \_\_return\_true by design.   Authentication is enforced by Helios at the edge.   Do not add WordPress auth to these endpoints. \#\# Environment variables and constants   SPX\_AI\_KEY         — Anthropic API key (required, in wp-config.php)   SPX\_MU\_PATH        — override mu-plugins path if needed \#\# Getting help   Read AGENTS.md for architectural reasoning.   Read .github/copilot-instructions.md for specific rules.   Read the Platform Integrity Map for cross-repo decisions.   See sparxstar-ouroboros-integrity for shared contracts and DTOs. |
| :---- |

# **Part 3 — Sirus Context — All Three Files**

The Sirus files incorporate the UEC production knowledge — the UEC plugin is the live predecessor that Sirus replaces via the StarUserEnv compatibility shim. The UEC copilot instructions Copilot generated contain accurate production knowledge that belongs in the Sirus documentation.

**✦  Sirus Context — .github/copilot-instructions.md**

File path: .github/copilot-instructions.md

| \# SPARXSTAR — sparxstar-sirus-context \# Copilot Instructions \#\# What this repository is Sirus is the context engine of the SPARXSTAR platform. Before identity is established, before authentication runs, before any application logic executes — Sirus establishes the environment. It determines what is happening right now: who is present, on what device, in what environment, under what authority. Sirus produces context. It does not make authorization decisions (Helios). It does not enforce governance (Mehns). \#\# Production context — UEC compatibility The original sparxstar-user-environment-check plugin is in production. Sirus replaces it via a namespace shim. The StarUserEnv facade class is the frozen public interface — its signatures must never change.   StarUserEnv::get\_browser\_name()             → ContextEngine::current()?-\>environment-\>browser\_name   StarUserEnv::get\_os()                       → ContextEngine::current()?-\>environment-\>os   StarUserEnv::get\_device\_type()              → ContextEngine::current()?-\>environment-\>device\_type   StarUserEnv::get\_network\_effective\_type()   → ContextEngine::current()?-\>environment-\>network\_effective\_type   StarUserEnv::get\_ip\_address()               → ContextEngine::current()?-\>environment-\>ip\_address   StarUserEnv::get\_location()                 → ContextEngine::current()?-\>environment-\>location \#\# Architecture   Orchestrator: SirusContext.php (singleton, wires services)   REST API:     SirusRESTController.php (context endpoint)   Database:     SirusDatabase.php (all SQL isolated here)   Public Facade: StarUserEnv.php (static API, cache-backed)   Asset Manager: AssetManager.php (enqueues JS/CSS)   Caching:      defaults to object cache (Redis required);                 fallback to per-request pulse verification only \#\# What this repository owns \- ContextEngine — current() accessor, CLI System Context \- SirusContext DTO — primary output, consumed by all downstream layers \- ContextPulse generation and HMAC-SHA256 signing (PulseGenerator) \- PulseVerifier — six-check canonical verification contract \- TrustEngine — trust state and score computation \- DeviceContinuity — server-issued device\_id, fingerprint, recovery \- DeviceMatcher — fingerprint scoring thresholds \- EnvironmentResolver — browser, OS, network via Matomo DeviceDetector \- IdentityResolver — five-tier resolution \- AuthorityResolver — governance scope, multi-authority aggregation \- ConsentManager — technical consent, purpose consent, history \- StepUpPolicy — Level 3 always, Level 2 when trust\_score \< 0.7 \- NetworkContextBroker — cross-domain handoff \- StarUserEnv — frozen public facade (UEC compatibility) \#\# What this repository does NOT own \- Agreement evaluation (proceed/deny) — Helios \- KV revocation — Helios \- Governance policy — Mehns \- Persistence — Dheghom \- Draft accumulation — Sky \- Pulse VERIFICATION — Sirus GENERATES. Helios VERIFIES. \#\# Hard rules \- declare(strict\_types=1) in every file \- Namespace: Starisian\\Sparxstar\\Sirus\\ \- Deployed as WordPress mu-plugin — cannot be deactivated \- MUST NEVER call wp\_set\_auth\_cookie() or issue JWTs \- MUST NEVER query Dheghom or any external plugin directly \- ContextEngine::current() returns valid SirusContext or throws   ContextBootException — never null, never partial \- ContextBootException MUST NEVER be caught and swallowed \- device\_id is ALWAYS server-issued — never JS fingerprint alone \- IP addresses stored with last octet zeroed: 192.168.1.0 \- ContextPulse NEVER contains identity claims \- ?-\> required wherever nullable is consumed (PHPStan Level 5\) \- StarUserEnv signatures are FROZEN — must never change \#\# Trust score algorithm (frozen)   base \= 1.0   device drifting:   \-0.3   geo mismatch:      \-0.2   new session:       \-0.1   recent failures:   \-0.3   clamped to \[0.0, 1.0\] \#\# CLI context (when PHP\_SAPI \=== "cli")   identity\_id  \= "SYSTEM"   trust\_score  \= 1.0   trust\_level  \= "NORMAL"   authority\_id \= "GLOBAL"   device\_id    \= "CLI" \#\# Filters (production — must remain stable)   sparxstar\_env\_cache\_handler       — switch cache backend   sparxstar\_env\_cache\_ttl           — set cache duration   sparxstar\_env\_geolocation\_ttl     — set geolocation cache duration   sparxstar\_env\_geolocation\_lookup  — custom geolocation provider   sparxstar\_env\_retention\_days      — snapshot retention \#\# Build and test   composer install   composer run lint       \# PHPCS PSR-12   composer run analyze    \# PHPStan Level 5   composer run test:unit  \# PHPUnit   composer test           \# full suite \#\# Dependencies — from sparxstar-ouroboros-integrity   ContextBootException   ContextPulse DTO   AgreementResult enum   ValidationHelper   Never redefine these. Use the Ouroboros package. |
| :---- |

**✦  Sirus Context — AGENTS.md**

File path: AGENTS.md

| \# SPARXSTAR Sirus Context — Agent Instructions \#\# Who reads this file AI agents operating in this repository. It provides reasoning beyond what copilot-instructions.md states. Read both. \#\# Platform position Sirus is the platform kernel. It loads first (as a mu-plugin). Everything else depends on what it produces.   Ouroboros → Helios → Sirus → Sky → Mehns → Dheghom Sirus is at position 2\. It receives nothing from downstream layers. It produces context that all downstream layers consume. \#\# The UEC migration — why this matters sparxstar-user-environment-check is in production on a live site. Sirus replaces it transparently via the StarUserEnv shim. Migration strategy: Transparent Proxy. \- Sirus boots and populates its environment record on every request. \- StarUserEnv proxies all calls to Sirus internals. \- No calling code changes. \- For the first 30 days post-deployment, fail-closed rules apply   only to new /sparxstar/ and /aiwa/ endpoints. \- After stabilisation, fail-closed rules extend to all governed paths. Do not break StarUserEnv. Its signatures are a permanent contract. \#\# Why the client is the source of truth for environment The original UEC architecture established this correctly: server-side User-Agent parsing is unreliable. Matomo DeviceDetector is used for UA parsing but the client-side signals (visitorId, canvas hash, screen resolution, timezone) are the primary fingerprint inputs. visitorId is client-side derived, probabilistic, used for drift detection only. It is NOT a security primitive. The server-issued device\_id is the real persistent identity. \#\# Why Sirus is a mu-plugin Sirus must establish context before any standard plugin, theme, or authentication hook can execute. A regular plugin can be deactivated. A mu-plugin cannot. The boot order is enforced by the loader:   mu-plugins/00-sparxstar-loader.php requires Sirus first. \#\# Why ContextBootException must never be swallowed If ContextEngine::current() throws and the exception is caught and ignored, every downstream decision operates on undefined state: \- Helios agreement uses an undefined device\_id \- Triple Binding in Dheghom uses an undefined session \- Governance evaluation uses an undefined authority There is no recovery from missing context. There is only halt. \#\# The hostile environment assumption Sirus runs in a full commercial WordPress ecosystem. Third-party plugins may hook into muplugins\_loaded, plugins\_loaded, and init at any priority. Sirus must establish context before any of them can interfere. This is why it boots at the highest priority via the loader. \#\# Cross-repo dependency check Before implementing any validation logic, search Ouroboros for ValidationHelper. All validation methods live there. Before implementing any exception, check Ouroboros exceptions. ContextBootException is defined in Ouroboros — do not redefine it. |
| :---- |

**✦  Sirus Context — INSTRUCTIONS.md**

File path: INSTRUCTIONS.md

| \# SPARXSTAR Sirus Context — Developer Instructions \#\# What this is Sirus is the context engine of the SPARXSTAR platform. It runs before identity, authentication, and application logic. It replaces the sparxstar-user-environment-check (UEC) plugin via a transparent compatibility shim. \#\# Prerequisites   PHP 8.2+   WordPress 6.8+   Composer 2.x   Redis (required — persistent object cache for trust state)   Matomo DeviceDetector (bundled via Composer, committed to vendor/) \#\# Setup   1\. This plugin loads as a mu-plugin via the platform loader.      Do not activate it as a standard plugin.   2\. Verify the loader file exists:        mu-plugins/00-sparxstar-loader.php      Sirus must be the first require\_once in that file.   3\. Install dependencies:        composer install      Note: vendor/ is committed. Do not gitignore it.      Matomo DeviceDetector is a committed dependency — no runtime fetch.   4\. Confirm Redis is available.      Without persistent object cache, trust cache is disabled.      System falls back to per-request pulse verification.      Transients must not be used for trust state. \#\# UEC compatibility If the site currently runs sparxstar-user-environment-check: \- Deploy Sirus first. Verify StarUserEnv methods return correct values. \- Keep UEC active for 30 days alongside Sirus (parallel shim mode). \- After 30 days, deactivate UEC. StarUserEnv continues via Sirus. StarUserEnv method signatures are frozen. If they return wrong values, fix the Sirus internals — never change the method signatures. \#\# Running tests   composer test           \# full suite   composer run lint       \# PHPCS PSR-12   composer run analyze    \# PHPStan Level 5   composer run test:unit  \# PHPUnit \#\# Available filters (stable — do not remove)   sparxstar\_env\_cache\_handler      — switch cache backend   sparxstar\_env\_cache\_ttl          — cache duration   sparxstar\_env\_geolocation\_ttl    — geolocation cache duration   sparxstar\_env\_geolocation\_lookup — custom geolocation provider   sparxstar\_env\_retention\_days     — snapshot retention period   sparxstar\_sirus\_device\_ttl\_days  — device record TTL (default 90\) \#\# Branching and PR rules   No direct commits to main.   PHPStan Level 5 must pass.   PHPCS PSR-12 must pass.   All PHPUnit tests must pass. \#\# Key architectural rule Sirus PRODUCES context. Helios EVALUATES whether a request proceeds. If you find yourself making a yes/no decision about a request inside Sirus code, stop. That decision belongs in Helios. |
| :---- |

# **Part 4 — Template Pattern for Remaining Repositories**

The following template shows the consistent structure every repository must follow. Apply this pattern to Ouroboros, Helios, Mehns, Dheghom, and the DVE Schema repository. The specific content differs per repo but the structure, section headings, and file names are identical across all seven.

## **Consistent structure — all three files, all seven repos**

| Section | copilot-instructions.md | AGENTS.md | INSTRUCTIONS.md |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Opening | What this repo is (2-3 sentences) | Who reads this \+ platform position | What this is \+ who should read what |
| Architecture | Diagram or ownership list | Why the architecture is this way | Not included |
| Owns | Bullet list of components | Not repeated | Not repeated |
| Does NOT own | Bullet list with cross-repo pointers | Reasoning for boundaries | Not included |
| Hard rules | Every rule as a bullet | Reasoning for key rules | Key rules only |
| Cross-repo deps | Symbols to use from Ouroboros | Why to search before defining | Setup steps |
| Build/test | Exact commands | Not included | Full setup \+ test guide |
| When uncertain | Decision tree | Deeper reasoning | Who to ask / where to look |

## **File naming — identical across all repos**

* .github/copilot-instructions.md — read by GitHub Copilot automatically

* AGENTS.md — read by Claude Code, other MCP-aware AI agents

* INSTRUCTIONS.md — read by human developers onboarding

## **The one line that belongs in every file in every repo**

Every copilot-instructions.md closes with this:

| \#\# When uncertain Check the Platform Integrity Map before adding anything to this repository. If the thing you are building is needed by only one component, it belongs in that component. If it crosses component boundaries, it belongs in Ouroboros. If it contradicts a rule in the Platform Integrity Map, the Map governs. |
| :---- |

*Code-derived knowledge from Copilot \+ architectural law from the Platform Integrity Map. Neither alone is complete. Both together give any agent or developer everything they need.*
